description_long: This is a standard manual screwdriver featuring a long, straight shaft with a pointed tip and a hexagonal yellow handle. The handle is made of yellow plastic with black inlays, providing both grip and visual contrast. The shaft appears to be made of metal, likely steel, and is dark in color, possibly coated for corrosion resistance. The tip is sharply defined and appears to be a Phillips type, suitable for cross-head screws. The handle is straight and faceted, allowing for a firm grip and preventing rolling on flat surfaces. There are no visible brand markings or signs of wear, and the overall appearance is clean and well-maintained.
description: A yellow and black straight-handled Phillips screwdriver with a metal shaft and pointed tip, appearing clean and undamaged.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the yellow handle and the metal shaft extending to a pointed tip.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, highlighting its hexagonal shape and black inlays.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the full length of the handle and shaft, with the tip pointing left.
description_view_3: Direct view of the tip, emphasizing the Phillips cross shape and the handle's end.
attribute_handle_type: straight, hexagonal, plastic, with black inlays for grip
